source_or_gap: Corben-authored Reflexive Router v1.2: pre-deliberative command and routing, qualification-first heterogeneous dispatch, effect commitment, typed temporal continuity, and governed reasoning-to-reflex compilation.
chapter_action_policy: Strengthen the twelve existing chapter owners through one coordinated roadmap package. Keep routing as the primary owner of the pre-deliberative event-to-qualified-plan boundary. Do not add a standalone Reflexive Router chapter unless experiments expose a stable interface, invariant, artifact, or failure class that the current owners cannot represent without authority conflict.
merge_or_split_policy: Merge command semantics, route admission, planning, capability substitution, typed context, temporal belief, effect commitment, procedural compilation, resource accounting, benchmark design, and integrated trace material into their existing owners. Reconsider a split only after P4 produces an observed unowned boundary.
boundary_decision_rationale: The paper's value is a disciplined ordering and contract synthesis across established ASI Stack layers. A paper-shaped chapter would duplicate those layers. The primary new experimental pressure is to test the joined pre-deliberative interface, not to create another narrative owner before evidence exists.
external_literature_need: current primary work on heterogeneous LLM/tool routing, cascades, selective prediction, calibrated abstention, and out-of-distribution detection
primary capability-security, effect-system, transaction, idempotency, and compensation references
primary temporal database, event-sourcing, provenance, and truth-maintenance references
primary cognitive-architecture production compilation, plan caching, procedural memory, and agent-skill compilation references
current independently reproducible routing and agent benchmarks with natural composite and effectful tasks
proof_or_test_backlog: implemented at finite design-contract scope: one schema-bound Reflexive Dispatch Trace joins canonical event, ingress command, route proposals, qualification receipts, execution nodes, effect custody, typed result, Chronicle update, metrics, and reflex lifecycle; no runtime behavior is implied
build one bounded reference slice covering automatic, forced-route, direct-command, and workflow dispatch through the same authority and verification kernels
test silent fallback, untrusted command text, parameter ambiguity, stale context, OOD routing, contract rejection, and authority widening
test idempotent effect retry, partial failure, compensation, observed postconditions, effect-complete rollback, and verifier disagreement
run a natural and adversarial ReflexBench-derived campaign across atomic, paraphrased, contextual, composite, temporal, effectful, adversarial, and compilation tracks
compare LLM-first, hard-rule, learned-router, cache, tool-agent, modular, and full reflexive policies under matched information, model, authority, retries, verifier access, and total cost

measure useful outcomes, wrong-fast-path rate, qualified coverage, selective risk, route regret, override fidelity, silent fallback, DAG validity, continuity, temporal accuracy, unauthorized effects, verification escape, rollback completeness, latency, and total cost
attempt trace-to-reflex compilation with negative-space guards, replay, differential checks, shadowing, drift, quarantine, rollback, and decompilation only after routing instrumentation is adequate
evidence_transition_precondition: claim-specific source and passage mapping is complete in every receiving chapter
the campaign uses prospective labels, immutable prompts and tasks, complete candidate denominators, and independently implemented route, outcome, and effect evaluators
candidate policies receive matched model, information, authority, retry, verification, and total-resource opportunities
positive, null, negative, narrowed, blocked, and refuted outcomes are retained with uncertainty and full cost accounting
an accepted claim-specific evidence transition is recorded before any support-state change
required_before_drafting: completed: normalized reflex, route, command, capability, workflow, plan, action proposal, effect, result, Chronicle record, and compiled procedure terminology across the receiving owners

completed: deduplicated the paper against QCSA, Octopus routing, Stable Capability Fields, command contracts, procedural memory, context ABI, claim ledger, runtime adapter, and integrated-trace material
preserved as a literature boundary: cited external literature must be passage-reviewed from current primary sources before novelty, security, or performance statements gain external support
completed: wrote chapter changes around explicit owner boundaries and one cross-layer trace rather than repeating a paper-shaped chapter
completed: preserved the proposal-versus-result boundary and did not import illustrative launch targets as measurements
promotion_blockers: the source is an architectural proposal and research agenda rather than an implementation or empirical report
no natural heterogeneous routing campaign has established useful fast-path coverage or lower route regret
no deployed command registry, qualification engine, Effect Commit Kernel, Temporal Chronicle, or Reflex Compiler has been observed
no independent evaluator or implementer has reproduced the proposed behavior
external prior-work, novelty, security, and benchmark claims have not been independently passage-reviewed for this intake
no claim-specific evidence transition has been accepted
residuals: the precise owner of the joined pre-deliberative event-to-qualified-plan artifact remains an empirical question
the paper's typed schemas are illustrative and require compatibility review against current ASI Stack protocols
Temporal Chronicle scope may overlap claim, artifact, context, and memory ledgers and needs one authority-preserving crosswalk
trace-to-reflex representation selection and verifier independence remain open research problems
raw DOCX/Markdown publication rights are not inferred from local ingestion
next_action: Execute the remaining P4 ReflexBench-derived natural and adversarial campaign only after the repaired routing instrument passes under new prospective authority; retain the completed twelve-chapter argument-only integration and finite trace contract as specification inputs, not campaign evidence.
non_claims: does not create a new chapter
does not implement or validate the Reflexive Router
does not establish routing accuracy, useful efficiency, safety, temporal truth, effect correctness, reflex transfer, or state of the art
does not promote a chapter-core claim
does not publish either raw source file
does not authorize a release, deployment, commit, push, or external publication
